headlines.title: Woman in hospital with suspected hantavirus infection; new case suspected on remote island
headlines.description: A Spanish woman who was on the same flight as a passenger who later died of hantavirus is being treated at a hospital for a suspected infection, local officials said Friday

headlines.title: Scientists are working on a hantavirus vaccine — but it’s likely years away
headlines.description: A hantavirus vaccine is currently in preclinical development, but a publicly available shot is likely years away. Other similar research efforts are also in the very early stages.

headlines.title: Florida surgeon who removed wrong organ says he is ‘forever traumatized’ by patient’s death
headlines.description: Thomas Shaknovsky said in a deposition in November that he believed he was removing 70-year-old William Bryan’s spleen, not his liver.

headlines.title: He pitched military service with a promise of lifetime healthcare. When he needed it, the system failed.
headlines.description: For more than a decade, Guy Shoemaker pitched military service to potential new recruits with one key promise: healthcare for life.“You’re going to have medical and dental for the rest of your life,” said Shoemaker, a retired Army sergeant first class and recruiter who spent a year in Afghanistan.

headlines.title: ‘A watershed moment’: A pancreatic cancer drug is set to transform treatment
headlines.description: An experimental pancreatic cancer drug that’s been shown to double survival in patients with advanced stages of the disease is poised to revolutionize the way the cancer is treated, oncologists say.

headlines.title: Rectal cancer deaths rising rapidly among millennials: 'It's a medical crisis'
headlines.description: Deaths from rectal cancer are rising rapidly among younger adults, an alarming trend that is confounding scientists trying to understand why millennials are so hard-hit.

headlines.title: What to Know About Cruise Ship Safety Amid Hantavirus Outbreak
headlines.description: The cruise ship hantavirus outbreak is sparking new health and safety concerns for cruises ahead of the busy summer travel season. While doctors say hantavirus infections are extremely rare and almost never transmitted human to human, other illnesses like norovirus and COVID can spread more easily where people spend a lot of time in close quarters. Reporting for TODAY, NBC’s Jesse Kirsch breaks down what you need to know if you are planning to book a cruise.

headlines.title: Officials Race to Track Hantavirus After Outbreak on Cruise Ship
headlines.description: The ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak is heading to the Canary Islands as health officials scramble to track passengers who disembarked from the ship early. It comes as new suspected cases are popping in multiple countries, including in the United Kingdom and Singapore. NBC’s Daniele Hamamdjian reports for TODAY from Tenerife.

headlines.title: Doctor who previously worked on cruise ship now experiencing hantavirus outbreak speaks out
headlines.description: NBC News' Tom Llamas talks to Dr. Martin Kriz, who previously worked on the cruise ship that now has an outbreak of hantavirus, about the health protocols and equipment on board.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.

headlines.title: Expert-approved walking pads that help you sit less and move more
headlines.description: Fitness trainers share the benefits of using walking pads daily for up to 60 mins. and safety tips. Plus, shop the best expert-approved walking pads.
